title: ProPublica Staff Strike Over AI, Layoffs, Wages
summary: ProPublica's unionized staff are on a 24-hour strike due to unresolved issues in collective bargaining. Key concerns include AI use, layoff protections, and wages.
body:
The ProPublica Guild, comprising around 150 members, began a 24-hour strike on Wednesday, calling for the public to support their digital picket line. This action comes amidst ongoing negotiations for a collective bargaining agreement, which the staff unionized for in 2023. 

 The strike is driven by several unresolved issues, including the need for protections around AI use, 'just cause' provisions for employee discipline or termination, layoff protections, and wage concerns. These issues have been under negotiation for over two years, with the union seeking fair resolutions. 

 The strike reflects the growing concerns of media workers regarding the impact of AI on their jobs and the need for stronger protections against unfair layoffs and discipline. As the situation unfolds, it remains to be seen how ProPublica management will respond to the union's demands and whether a resolution can be reached soon.
twitterThread: Breaking: ProPublica's unionized staff are on strike over unresolved issues including AI use, layoff protections, and wages. Support their digital picket line today!
The strike highlights growing concerns in the media industry about the impact of AI on jobs and the need for fair labor practices.
ProPublica's union has been negotiating for over two years, seeking protections for employees in the face of AI integration and potential layoffs.
As the media landscape evolves, the fight for workers' rights and fair labor standards continues. Stay tuned for updates on the ProPublica strike.
The use of AI in newsrooms raises important questions about job security, bias, and accountability. The ProPublica strike brings these issues to the forefront.
